title: Ormosia flava
taxonomy: Ormosia flava is a species of flowering plant in the family Leguminosae, commonly known as the pea family. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and the tribe Millettieae. Further classification places it in the genus Ormosia, which is a group of tropical trees and shrubs native to Asia and the Pacific Islands. Ormosia flava is a small tree or shrub found in various regions, including India and Southeast Asia. Its classification is a part of ongoing botanical research and may be subject to change as new information becomes available.

intro: Ormosia flava, also known as the yellow Ormosia, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Leguminosae. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Ormosia flava include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Ormosia flava is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and its root bark is often used to make teas, infusions, or capsules for its medicinal uses.
